title: Re: VSM Loop: Viable-System Orchestration for Multi-Ag...
text:
I guess noone would ever pay any attention but this thing works wonders (even its sub-skills are very valuable). It turns Claude/Codex into persistently working machines with much higher output quality - and without need for a custom harness (which actually could improve token usage, quality and discipline but comes with maintenance costs).

With these skills Claude can work completely autonomously for days and weeks only interrupting the flow when some input is genuinely required from the user.

A sandbox and yolo mode are must.

The theory behind, unfortunately, doesn't have the recognition it deserves.
author: pshirshov

title: Show HN: Catio - AWS Diagrams and Architecture Copilot
text:
Hey HN, I’m working on Catio, a tool that builds live digital twin diagrams of your AWS infrastructure and includes an AI copilot that can answer questions and reason against that model.

The diagram and chat experience is free to use, read-only, and can be connected to dev or sandbox AWS accounts if you want to try it without touching production. We also have a paid product around AI-generated recommendations and architecture designs, but currently I’m mostly looking for feedback on if this feels useful to you, and how we can improve it!

Happy to answer any questions in thread :)
author: sirmilesdu

title: Re: Apparently Google hates us now...
text: After yesterday's keynote and the changes to Search, it became clear in the near future, Google will cease to direct any traffic to websites and the search results will just become a footnote in Gemini's response.
author: chakintosh

title: Re: Stopgap for Claude -p before Anthropic's June 15 b...
text:
Anthropic is moving claude -p to per-token API billing on June 15 — interactive sessions stay subscription-billed. If you have any pipelines that use claude -p today, they'll start generating API charges.

I just thought of a quick stopgap — a ~50-line Python wrapper that replicates claude -p's one-shot behavior using an interactive session instead.

The trick is getting the interactive session to self-terminate after it produces output — the gist handles that plus timeout/retry since LLMs don't always follow instructions reliably.

Gist (tested on macOS, but should work on Linux): https://gist.github.com/HammerMei/8ceef2740cf094188e1383fce0... Write-up: https://dev.to/hammermei/claude-p-alternative-for-cicd-a-50-...

It's not a long-term solution — but may be helpful to get you through the transition. For my own Claude integration, I ended up having to build something heavier to move away from claude -p.
author: hammer-mei

title: ChatGPT and other AI bots made errors before Scottish election, study finds
author: chrisjj

title: Re: I Caught ChatGPT for Finance Making Up a Transacti...
text:
Drives me crazy when someone identifies a bad inconsistency in output like this and says "OpenAI should look into this issue", like it's not a core limitation of this technology at a foundational level.

That's what scares me the most about this stuff. People see the generated output and get wowed by it, then notice the issues and think "they'll get that problem ironed out", when the truth is that we're gonna probably be dealing with some form of hallucination forever. I'm not convinced at all that we're gonna get past this problem, we're just gonna get more comfortable with letting other people find the bad output.
author: CodingJeebus

title: Show HN: Sticky Icky – a ditherpunk sticker designer for the Zebra LP2844
text:
Browser-based sticker designer for a Zebra LP2844 thermal label printer. I bought an $40 UPS branded LP2844 to print stickers and ended up building this after I couldn't find existing tools that did what I wanted.

GW raster appears to be broken over USB on firmware V4.29, so it prints over serial from a FastAPI backend. More notes on what I ran into are in the README. Image pipeline does XOR compositing, EPX upscale, Otsu/manual threshold, and five dithering algorithms (Bayer 4x4/8x8, Floyd-Steinberg, Atkinson, Riemersma).

Part of this was just an experiment to see if I could use Claude to diagnose the print issues I was running into. Some of the conclusions in the project may be entirely wrong, or entirely specific to my setup. I've arrived at a workflow that works for me but may be based on wrong assumptions. Would love for someone else with an LP2844 to test it out.
author: sigspec
